Question ID: coding-S3-ahc024-topological-map
Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map
Family: coding

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Submitted final result text:
FINAL_ANSWER: Use a Steiner Tree-based placement strategy combined with Simulated Annealing optimization.
JUSTIFICATION: A Steiner Tree approach minimizes the total number of cells required to connect all color-adjacency relations (nodes), effectively reducing the footprint of the map. Simulated Annealing is then used to iteratively refine the placement, allowing the system to escape local optima to maximize the number of empty cells (E) while strictly adhering to connectivity and adjacency constraints.
